improvement.summary: Bind Cloudflare access readiness to the exact mirror-cartographer-research Pages project and its canonical public hostname.
improvement.problem: The previous probe treated generic permission to enumerate Pages projects as deployment readiness, even when the intended project was absent or had no canonical hostname.
behavior: Require exact target project name match.
Normalize and emit only public canonical and custom hostnames.
Reject generic Pages access as target readiness.
Reject a found project that lacks a canonical hostname as unresolved identity.
Never emit API tokens or account identifiers.

covered_cases: exact target project and canonical hostname accepted
generic Pages access without target rejected
target project without canonical hostname rejected
rejected token stops before project lookup
malformed configuration rejected before network access
verification.strongest_observed_evidence: Committed source and deterministic Node test contract; GitHub reported no workflow runs for aac1a5d9ff167c3ac2a2a339a2eb959ffb310aa3.

limits: A canonical hostname returned by the Cloudflare API does not by itself prove that the current source commit is served there.
Deployment proof still requires a successful workflow run, returned deployment URL, served identity verification, and commit-bound attestation.
The tests were committed but not executed in this run.
